NCT01772472.name: KATHERINE
NCT01772472.group: Trastuzumab Emtansine (T-DM1) 3.6 mg/kg IV q3w x14 | ADC (HER2+ EBC with residual invasive disease post-neoadjuvant; vs Trastuzumab 6 mg/kg q3w x14)

allOutcomes.title: Invasive disease-free survival T-DM1 vs trastuzumab (KATHERINE primary; 3-yr iDFS 88.3% vs 77.0%)

NCT01772472.snippet: von Minckwitz G et al. NEJM 2019;380:617-628 (KATHERINE). Practice-changing adjuvant T-DM1 for residual-disease patients; established ADC standard of care in EBC.

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; min age 18 Years; healthy volunteers: f. CRITERIA: "Inclusion Criteria:~* Adult patient, >/= 18 years of age~* HER2-positive breast cancer~* Histologically confirmed invasive breast carcinoma~* Clinical stage T1-4/N0-3/M0 at presentation (patients with T1a/bN0 tumors will not be eligible)~* Completion of preoperative systemic chemotherapy and HER2-directed treatment consisting of at least 6 cycles of chemotherapy with a total duration of at least 16 weeks, including at least 9 weeks of trastuzumab and at least 9 weeks of taxane-based therapy~* ...

NMA_CONFIG.note: k=1 (pairwise; no additional HER2-ADC adjuvant pivotal trials published). Can extend in v2 if DESTINY-Breast05 (T-DXd adjuvant) reads out.

title: RapidMeta Oncology | ADC NMA in HER2+ Early Breast Cancer Adjuvant (Residual Disease) v1.0
